Details: Removal of perchlorate by membrane separation is very effective (up to greater than 95 percent removal). Membrane separation technologies include reverse osmosis (RO), nanofiltration (NF), electrodialysis (ED), and electrodialysis reversal (EDR). The EDR units are usually made of ion exchange resins and the NF and RO membranes were mostly made out of polyamide materials. The cost, residual stream issues, and possible distribution system impacts of high-pressure membrane systems makes this technology difficult to justify unless other difficult-to-remove contaminants such as arsenic, pesticides, and DBP precursors are present. The presence of silica may make ED/EDR cost effective as compared to the pressure driven processes.
